The Official BNI Podcast

A weekly audio discussion with Dr. Ivan Misner, the Founder and Chief Visionary Officer of BNI, the world's largest business networking organization.

Episode 543: How to Use the BNI Podcast

January 31, 2018 by Ivan Misner 37 Comments

Podcast: Download

Subscribe: Apple Podcasts | RSS | Subscription Options

Print This Episode

Synopsis

In business, if you really sit down and think about what you’re going to do and how you’re going to do it, you tend to be much more successful. Dr Misner came up with 9 different things to think about when preparing to listen to The Official BNI podcast, and Priscilla adds a suggestion.

BNI Members

  • Listen in your office and take notes (or print the transcripts and highlight them).
  • Listen to the podcasts in your car. Anything you can listen to and learn when you’re in traffic is awesome.
  • Download the podcasts from iTunes and listen to them on your mobile device.
  • Listen to podcasts when you’re doing the dishes, walking the dog, or exercising.

Education Coordinators

  • Education coordinators can provide short summaries of an episode or play a clip from one.
  • Hand out a transcript at a meeting and talk about it.
  • Assign specific podcasts based on what the group needs and discuss them at the next meeting.
  • Use the search function on the BNI Podcast website to find the episodes most relevant to you.
  • Assign podcasts to new members or those who are struggling with part of the BNI program.

BNI Directors

  • BNI Directors should be assigning podcasts to people who are working on developing networking skills.

Some Podcast Stats as of January 2nd, 2018

There are 538 published episodes, minus 78 rebroadcasts = 460 unique episodes.

Each episode is approximately 10 minutes long. 460 x 10 = 4600 minutes, or 76 hours and 40 minutes of listening time–and that’s a conservative estimate.

Listening to every single episode is a marathon task, but you can sort podcasts by category, tag, or series to find the ones that interest you most.

If you have more ideas about how to listen to the BNI Podcast, let us know in the comments. And thank you for listening!

Brought to you by the Networking for Success Channel on YouTube.

Complete Transcript of Episode 543 –

Priscilla:
Hello everybody and welcome back to the Official BNI Podcast, brought to you by the Networking for Success Channel on YouTube, which features Dr. Ivan Miser and many other networking experts. I am Priscilla Rice, and I am coming to you from Live Oak Recording Studio in Berkeley, California. I am joined on the phone today by the Founder and the Chief Visionary Officer of BNI, Dr. Ivan Misner. Hello, Ivan, how are you today?

Ivan:
I am doing fantastic, Priscilla, thank you. We have an interesting topic, I think, today, how to use a BNI Podcast, how to listen to BNI Podcast. I started thinking about it. Sallie Goetch, the person who manages the BNI Podcast website, answered somebody’s question. She started throwing out some stats. I thought this is interesting. I started thinking about ways people could listen and how you could use the podcast as a result of it. I am going to share those stats with you at the end of this podcast.

I came up with a list of eight different things to think about as you listen to the podcast and what you can do with the podcast. Priscilla, we haven’t talked about this much, but if you have something to add to it, I would love to hear that. I think this is a great podcast to kind of – I have found that in business if you really sit down and think about what you are going to do and how you are going to do it, you will be more successful at doing it. This is kind of contemplating how to effectively use this amazing content.

We have hundreds and hundreds of podcasts, Priscilla, that you and I put together over the years. So here is my list. I have eight. Feel free to add any at the end here.

First, personally, if I were listening to the content, I would listen in my office and take notes. I like taking notes on stuff. That helps me think things through and try to apply them So that is number one for me. I recommend that members listen in their office and take a few notes or at the very least, print out the podcasts. This is one and a half. Print out the podcasts and highlight the things that you find interesting.

The real obvious one, Priscilla, is listen to them in your car. You know, I read BNI in Los Angeles for 30 years. I rode a lot, driving in LA, and the podcasts- anything you can listen to and learn in traffic is awesome, so listening to them in the car is number two.

Number three is a lot of people don’t know you can download the podcasts on iTunes. So listen to them on your device. I have a friend, Tony, in San Demis who downloads them on his mobile device and listens to them in his car as he is driving or plays them at his office or he plays them in between meetings. So downloading them on iTunes is number three.

Number four, Education Coordinators, I think this is the perfect tool for them. They can do summaries of the podcast. I have even heard of some Education Coordinators playing a short segment. You know, the podcasts are ten to twelve minutes or so. You can’t play the whole podcast, but you might play a little segment out of it. I have seen Education Coordinators do that.

Number five is to hand out the transcript at the meeting. Talk about it. Have people review and talk about different elements of it. I think that is a great way to do it as you are going over the podcast. Of course, you can assign a specific podcast based on the information you need. So figure out what the chapter needs and assign specific podcasts for dialogue at a BNI meeting.

There is a function on the podcast that you can play around with. That is the search function. The search function will help really hone down your search for any particular topic that you are looking for. How do I invite visitors or networking techniques or some particular topic. The search function is – oftentimes, a lot of people ask, ” Do you have a podcast on [such and such]?” I say, “Did you do a search?” “No.” “Well, that is what I would do.” “Yeah?” Yeah. That is what I would do. The search function is number seven.

Number eight, assign podcasts to your members. You may be mentoring a member. If a member is struggling with something, a podcast can help. Or if they are just new to BNI and they need some ideas or techniques more effectively, assign the podcast.

I am going to add a ninth one. I just thought of this one for BNI Director Consultants and Directors. Directors should be assigning podcasts for people they are working with leadership teams as well. Assigning podcasts just like a mentor would to people developing their networking skills.

Those are the eight or nine I came up with, Priscilla. Can you think of any effective uses for the podcast?

Priscilla:
You know, I do listen to the podcasts on my mobile device and I do suggest that you can listen to podcasts when you are just doing the dishes or you are doing something mundane that doesn’t take your full attention. It really opens your mind to ideas.

Ivan:
That is a great suggestion. Listen to the podcast while you are doing the dishes or doing something mundane. You can listen to podcasts if you are walking on a treadmill or walking in the neighborhood. That is a great one. Good suggestion.

Priscilla:
They are not that long that you can actually get a whole one in a short period of time, or several if you are on the treadmill.

Ivan:
If you have another one, feel free to share it. I want to share Sallie’s content in a second. If you are listening to this podcast, I want you to post your ideas. We just came up with nine or ten. If you have an idea on how to use the podcast effectively at BNI, post it up here. I would love to see it. Do you have any others, Priscilla?

Priscilla:
I just think it is a real opportunity to listen to them in general to hear what you have to say, so- I mean. It is such a dynamic glowing resource, basically, so I just encourage everybody to listen to them.

Ivan:
So we have a member, Carl, who asked a question that Sallie Goetch happened to answer. Again, Sallie is the person who manages BNI Podcast website. This was her response, Priscilla:

“I am dusting off my grade school math. The podcasts are roughly ten minutes.” I thought everyone listening to this might find this interesting. I certainly did. “The podcasts are roughly ten minutes, usually a little longer. There are over 540 published episodes, but according to her trusty episode spreadsheet, 78 of them are rebroadcast.” By the way, we rebroadcast classic ones because they are great content and I had been concerned they would get buried. Podcast number 97. Who is going to go back? So we rebroadcast some of the classics. “78 of them are rebroadcast. We have 540-78 for a total of 462 unique podcasts. If you multiply that by 10 – which is conservative. by the way – you have 4,620 minutes of podcasts. 77 hours of listening. I wouldn’t recommend staying awake for three days to do that.” Yes, I would agree with that, Sallie.”Put it another way, if you listened to two a day, it would take over 230 days to hear all of the current episodes. Forget about the new ones coming out.” Just the current episodes.

230 days for just the current episodes. Wow. That is just mind boggling. The bank of information that we have created is amazing. If you are listening to this, share with your BNI members if they are not listening to this podcast, they need to come over and check it out because there is some fantastic content.

Let me wrap up by saying, however, in response to Carl’s question all the podcasts contain terrific information. You might want to listen to episodes on certain topics first. Then go back and fill things in. Most episodes are independent so you can listen to them on your own. You can sort the episodes by category, tag or series, as well as search. You can [sort by] category, tag or series and get list of shows based on that. The categories list is on the side bar.

Here is the last thing she said, which I think is important. “You may wish to download some of the bundles of episodes.” We have done bundles “-by going to BNIPodcast.com/shows-by-year.” We will put the link actually in this podcast so you can go there and get bundles.

So great content and thanks for your addition, Priscilla. Sallie, we have never talked about you on the podcast. It is about time. Thank you for that addition. If you have some ideas on how to effectively use BNI Podcast, please share them with us here on the comments section.

Thanks a lot, Priscilla. Back to you.

Priscilla:
Last of all, I just want to say thank you all for being listeners because we do it for you and it is just wonderful to have you out there.

Ivan:
It is, and I am glad you just added that in, Priscilla.

Priscilla:
Okay, well, that is it for this week. Thank you so much, Dr. Misner for bringing this little topic up. I would just like to remind the listeners that this podcast has been brought to you by the Networking for Success Channel on YouTube. Thank you for listening. This is Priscilla Rice and we look forward to having you join us again next week for another exciting episode of the Official BNI Podcast.

ShareTweetLinkedinWhatsappSaveSendFlipboard

Posted in Getting The Most From BNI

Comments

    Solutions-focused comments are welcome. Dr. Misner reads every comment personally.

    All comments are moderated before posting. Profanity, personal attacks, and sales pitches will be deleted.

  1. Jon Upleger says

    January 31, 2018 at 5:16 am

    Great conversation! I would also add listen to them more than once, especially if it’s an area you, your members, or your chapter is struggling.

    Reply

  2. Kenley Stringer says

    January 31, 2018 at 5:17 am

    I’ve found it can be helpful to others (and also incite some interest in BNI) to share topic-specific podcasts with potential guests and visitors. Of course you want to target the person with the content…send them something meaningful that shows you were listening when they mentioned a problem in their business, lead generation, relationships, etc. They may or may not listen to it but it’s another way to spread the word and show that you care about the individual.

    Reply

  3. Beth Ruck says

    January 31, 2018 at 8:12 am

    Here are several ways I use BNI podcasts:

    I am the mentor coordinator for my chapter. When a new person joins, I give them a list of the Podcasts that deal with Get Connected, Stay Connected with BNI Connect.

    I also give new members a list of all of the Podcasts by number and title. This way they can look at the list and see if any topics are of particular interest to them and then they can go to BNI podcasts, type in the number and listen to it.

    If I listen to a BNI podcast that I think a business colleague of mine (who is not in BNI) would find interesting, I share the podcast with them.

    Thank you for all the great information on the podcasts.

    Reply

  4. Shannon Walsh says

    January 31, 2018 at 11:01 am

    We send a list of “How to earn/where to find CEUs” every few weeks to the chapter lovingly titled “CEU Extravaganza”. We have found that giving the specifics of where to find and how to access podcasts has increased member usage. I’ll add the suggestions from this podcast to the list.

    My only complaint is that I only have access to the last year or so of podcasts on my phone. I’ve tried both Google Play and Podcast Addict and found the same limited playlist in both. I am in my car a lot and it would be great to have access to all of the podcasts for streaming.

    Reply

    • Sallie Goetsch says

      January 31, 2018 at 1:11 pm

      Hi, Shannon. Unfortunately, when there are too many items in an RSS feed (which is what Google Play, Podcast Addict, Apple Podcasts, etc. use to import the episodes), it can bring those apps to a grinding halt, leaving people unable to download any episodes at all. Though it requires a bit of work, you can download earlier episodes from the Shows By Year page and transfer them to your phone manually.

      Reply

  5. Mimi Amos says

    February 1, 2018 at 5:43 am

    Here’s the thing. If you’ve got 15 minutes to spend on Facebook, you’ve got time to listen to one podcast ? Use the iTunes podcast app and stick your phone near you as you do home chores, clean up your desk, take a shower, using the treadmill etc….Personally I knock out several when I mow or work in the chicken coop!?

    Reply

    • Ivan Misner says

      February 1, 2018 at 3:06 pm

      Mimi, your first two lines made me laugh out loud!

      Thanks everyone for the great comments.

      Ivan

      Reply

  6. Jeff Nies says

    February 1, 2018 at 5:56 am

    Great episode.
    I am Director Consultant and every chapter I visit is a hour drive or more so I listen to a lot of pod cast on the drive. With each chapter visit there is always a little tip or piece of pertinent information that I pick up from the podcast on my drive that I share and either my education moment or my featured presentation to the chapter. Enjoy the podcast very much they’re a great education tool and I promote them at all of my chapters.

    Reply

  7. Tony Dalia says

    February 1, 2018 at 10:28 am

    Thanks for mentioning me on this weeks podcast. I was listening to it on the way to my BNI meeting. I want to thank you Ivan for all of the content. I want to thank Priscilla for your calming voice. I want to thank Sally for putting up with all of my emails about links and functionality.

    Reply

  8. Ruth says

    February 2, 2018 at 4:56 am

    I listen on my way to my BNI meetings. My drive is about 15 minutes or less so it is the perfect time to listen to a podcast and it gets my mind ready and in the BNI mindframe so that I am ready for the meeting!

    Reply

  9. Tiffanie Kellog says

    February 2, 2018 at 5:25 am

    I remember back when the podcast just started, and it was certainly easier to listen as they came out new…
    but the idea of only having 76 hours worth of content, we can binge-listen very easily!
    It’s like netflix for audios!

    Reply

  10. Link Wachler says

    February 4, 2018 at 10:29 am

    Good topic. These podcasts have allowed me to understand why we do what we do in BNI. I feel I have gotten to know the heart of Ivan Misner as well as Priscilla and consider them friends who want to help me succeed. I have listened to the podcasts backwards to 2007 and forwards and have been able to understand the evolution of BNI over the past 11 years. By listening over and over again, I always pick up so something I haven’t heard. As an Arrtist/Jewelry Designer, I listen while in my studio creating, where I find that is the best environment for me to to hear and retain information. I’m in my zone. Along with all the great information from these podcasts, I also find them to be relational. I have learned about the BNI Foundation founded by Ivan and Beth Misner, the health struggles that both Ivan and Beth have gone through and have been inspired by their public openness and courage. I constantly encourage my chapter to listen. Through these podcasts they can also understand the WHY of BNI. Keep up the good work Ivan and Priscilla.
    Link

    Reply

  11. Vadim Pantall says

    February 4, 2018 at 5:25 pm

    I knew that BNI was a critical success factor for my business, so it was a no brainer to try and learn everything I could about BNI. Once I learnt during the Diamond Growth program that there was a gold mine of information in the podcasts, I averaged about 50-60 a week until I’d listened to them all.
    I still listen to about 20-40 a week depending on how much driving I do, and revisit them to be able to go-deeper embedding the learnings to memory, making notes or actions from it.
    I can’t wait for more of the BNI books to get made to Audio books, as I even started recording myself reading them out to be able to revisit them while driving (lots of open roads here in Australia!)
    Thanks
    Vadim Pantall – BNI Western Australia

    Reply

  12. Vadim Pantall says

    February 4, 2018 at 6:25 pm

    Who doesn’t have 5 minutes on their commute to or from work each day?
    If you do, and use it for a podcast, you’ve now gotten your 1 x CEU credit for the week (5minutes each way, 5 working days a week – 5 x 10 minute podcasts = 1 x CEU credit)
    …and as I’ve given the podcasts downloaded onto a thumbdrive as a door prize to BNI chapter members before, I’ve worked out the total exact numbers as follows:
    * Official podcasts total running time at 97 hours and 13 minutes (or about a hundred x CEU credits)
    * Power of One podcasts with US National director Tim Roberts total running time of 50 hours and 47 minutes (approx. another fifty CEU credits).
    Assuming a member attends on average one MSP, one LT training reads one BNI book and listens to the BNI CD / reads the Success net emails 5 times in each year, that leaves 35 CEU credits each 12 month period for Podcasts (1 credit for 5 podcasts = 175 podcasts per year)
    This will then mean just doing the minimum of our BNI Core Value – lifelong learning, each member who has been active in BNI for 6 years should have finished all the official podcasts, plus many of the new ones.
    The resources are a goldmine that is right there for the taking, and we don’t even need to dig a huge hole to get the gold out. Thanks to the efforts of everyone to put the podcasts out there for our benefit.
    Vadim Pantall – BNI Albany Success – in Regional Western Australia

    Reply

    • Colleen Heidecker says

      February 6, 2018 at 6:12 pm

      This is amazing! Thanks for breaking it down like this! I work alone in a kitchen so I have them playing while I am working – makes me less lonely and I’m learning and I’m getting my CEU’s – all awesome things!

      Reply

      • Vadim Pantall says

        February 7, 2018 at 9:30 am

        Perfect. I also listen when hanging out the kids clothes on the outside clothes line. At least one per load of washing – or more if I listen at x1.5 speed!

  13. Martina berry says

    February 5, 2018 at 2:47 pm

    I am just new to BNI but I am loving the podcasts I listen to one on the way to the gym in the morning and one on the way home .. it’s become just as much a habit as going to the gym :) I am loving them !!! Great content in everyone I have listened to so far Thank you

    Reply

  14. Jackie Coombs says

    February 6, 2018 at 5:40 am

    I subscribe and download to my iPhone. I’m not a morning person and my BNI group meets at 8:00am. I use Ivan’s podcast to rev me up and get me going on Tuesday mornings! It always works. Being a Real Estate Broker, I’m in my car A LOT, I love that the episodes are short enough that I can listen to one or two on my way to an appointment or to and from work. Thank you, Ivan and Priscilla for the podcasts. They are great. (I also go to “search” anytime I have a question about any BNI subject.) Have a wonderful day! #refer #refer #refer

    Reply

  15. Mark Appelbaum says

    February 6, 2018 at 6:10 am

    I’m a Director Consultant in Utah and I love listening while I’m driving to chapters.
    Not that you want to plan for this but if the Feature presenter is a “no show” have your chapter listen to a podcast during the 10min spot.
    Also, not something you want to plan for but for those smaller chapters out there who may have extra time at the end, fill in the time with podcasts about growing and inviting.
    Thank you Ivan!

    Reply

    • Mary Damiani says

      February 14, 2018 at 4:54 am

      Great ‘fill in’ ideas

      Reply

  16. Colleen Heidecker says

    February 6, 2018 at 6:29 pm

    I love the podcasts! I joined BNI about 10 months ago and I just started listening to them in earnest a few weeks ago. I’m working my way through all the episodes – doing some of the new ones and mixing in the old ones. I’ll meet somewhere in the middle at some point!

    For me, it gets me pumped for my week and thinking about all the things that I can do to contribute to my Chapter. Since I work alone in a kitchen (I’m a Chocolatier) – I can do marathon listening and get in 20 or more episodes while I’m working.

    I am wondering – I was told that “we don’t count podcasts as CEU’s”. But, in listening to these I see that most people/Chapters do….wonder why this is something I was told? I’m listening to them regardless, there’s so much to learn!!

    Thanks for all that you do!

    Reply

  17. Summer Highfill says

    February 6, 2018 at 7:18 pm

    My BNI chapter coupled the weekly ed presentations with a “CEU Coordinator” position that created a monthly ed moment series with a matching weekly podcast. It kept the theme (and the CEUs) moving upwards each month and the ability to extend the message of the chapter meeting throughout the week was fantastic for our chapter to grow in a targeted tracking area.

    Reply

  18. Oanh Henry says

    February 7, 2018 at 6:56 pm

    I love the idea of sharing a topic from the podcast at the shareholders meetings, at the B2B meetings, and so on. It is a great way to build stronger relationships with your business partners and share similar vision. Thank you Ivan for your thoughtfulness and your level of energy to keep on sharing. Oanh Henry

    Reply

  19. Hal Corbin says

    February 10, 2018 at 4:57 am

    Hi everyone
    I have listened to pretty much all the podcast as I have been in BNI for just over 11 years now.
    I usually listen to them when I take a shower, so that’s 2 podcast a day.
    I have even started from #1 and an working my way through them again.
    I have found that listening to them has allowed me to refocus on some basic and key elements to implement in my business

    Reply

  20. Gregory Rocha says

    February 12, 2018 at 8:07 pm

    This is a great resource for education coordinators as you mentioned. I wish I listened to this podcast when I first started being education coordinator this term. I will make sure we end strong this term, and make sure our new education coordinator takes over with this information!

    Reply

  21. Robin van der Basch says

    February 13, 2018 at 4:59 am

    It is great to listen on your smartphone when you are walking the dogs. This is how I use the podcasts and it is a great inspirational tool to use as an educator and DC. I only recently heard about the podcast channel so there’s a lot of catching up to do.

    Reply

  22. Ruth Goodman says

    February 19, 2018 at 7:24 am

    I joined BNI last July. After a few weeks I discovered the ease of downloading and listening to the podcasts. I started with episode 1 and went all the way through. By December I had listened to all of them and have been awarded “Most CEUs” of my chapter for five months now. I listen to them in the car, on the treadmill, and sometimes at work.

    Last September, our area experienced Hurricane Irma. My family was in a mandatory evacuation zone and stayed at a friend’s house with 9 other people. The stress was very high and my escape was to go in to a room by myself and listen to podcasts. I was swept away to a place with no storm surges or 100 mph winds. It was a peaceful respite from the storm. Plus, I earned CEUs while we were evacuated!

    Thank you for these podcasts. After listening to 540+ of them, I feel that I have a firm grasp of the BNI philosophy. And it helps me help other new people in our group.

    Reply

    • Lancy Galbao says

      February 19, 2018 at 10:34 pm

      Wow! This is an inspiration to me in Mumbai, India. Thank you. The sailorman

      Reply

  23. Shawn Coopwood says

    April 2, 2018 at 8:00 pm

    If you are meeting with a client or prospect you can also send them the podcast or direct them to BNI podcasts. BNI is a great way to show people you have resources to grow their business as well as yours. The podcasts can be shown to potential members as well as to what someone has access to as a member. I have listened from 1-543 of the podcasts and I see the education as one of the highest values of joining BNI. Were else could you gain insight from some of the top business leaders in the world for pennies on the dollar. I would have paid the membership price just for the education through podcasts, entrepreneur.com, BNI university and books.

    Reply

  24. Lori White says

    April 10, 2018 at 4:50 am

    I listen to two or three podcasts when I’m at the gym.

    Reply

  25. Carl Justesen says

    April 30, 2018 at 8:19 pm

    Hello, Carl here. I got a kick out of this podcast. I remember that response when I wrote you telling you about my goal regarding listening to all the podcasts. I considered it a challenge which I was handling quite well until you guys got me started with the BNI University. Keep up the great work. P.S. I am also the same guy that walked up and introduced myself in Utah with “Hello Ivan. How are you and where are you.”

    Reply

  26. George Barnett says

    August 22, 2018 at 8:10 pm

    I get to listen to podcasts on an every day basis. To help our chapter, I’ve compiled playlists of similar themed podcasts. I burn a CD for each theme, such as a disc for “New Members” or “More Referrals”. The CDs now number around 17 and our chapter has a library to choose from for helping members earn CEUs and become better networkers.

    I’ve shared the playlist with many other chapters throughout the United States. Be happy to pass it along for those interested in starting a library for their chapter.

    An example, “New Member” CD consists of Episodes: 403,308, 417, 419, 447, 454

    Already have a new one from recent podcasts. “Meeting Etiquette”

    Reply

  27. Zitzi Martínez says

    September 26, 2018 at 12:53 am

    Esta herramienta-acervo cultural BNI es muy importante, sin embargo hasta ahora conozco gracias a este podcast que se encuentran organizados por tema y ello es un plus para ir directo al punto que necesito fortalecer o conocer.

    Reply

  28. Martin Culbert says

    June 25, 2019 at 7:22 pm

    I am an avid podcast listener. These have not only upped my game, our chapter is starting to share and talk about our favorite podcasts now. (#574… look it up)

    Here’s a download process to lighten your workload and make you more efficient.
    1. Download 5 podcasts to your mobile app.
    2. Listen to those 5.
    3. Delete the first 5 and download 5 more podcasts.
    4. Claim a CEU on BNI Connect.
    5. Rinse, Lather, Repeat.

    Reply

  29. Joe Whitcomb says

    August 15, 2019 at 6:21 pm

    First, listening while I lift is a great way to stay focused. Also, I have started to share the ideas I learn here with my legal staff. This week I shared Episode 528: How Many Networks and How Many Hours? with my staff. I told them that if they deployed the lessons they learned here, they wouldn’t have to work for me for very much longer, but they’d still want to.

    Reply

  30. Michael M. Nishizaw says

    April 19, 2020 at 1:29 pm

    For me its what I call “back to basics” training. Do it for my real estate business as we many times get caught up with the current business issues and forget about simple basics of the business. Love the podcast!

    Reply

  31. Shahbaz says

    June 3, 2020 at 1:10 am

    Thank you for sharing this. I would add that we could listen to this in power groups or contact spheres.

    Reply

Products


  • Networking Is a Marathon, Not a Sprint     Learn how to Outlast, Outconnect, and Outperform the competition, through patience, consistency, and a giver’s mindset.

  • Infinite Legacy: The 7 Principles of Creating a Lasting Impact With Your Life

  • The 3rd Paradigm: A Radical Shift to Greater Success

  • Who's in Your Room? Revised and Updated: The Question That Will Change Your Life

  • Work Your Network With The 4Cs: Turn Every Handshake Into a Steady Stream of Referrals

  • The Connector Effect: The Proven Way to Grow Your Business Right Now

  • Infinite Giving: The 7 Principles of Givers Gain

  • The Networking Mentor: Whose Story Are You In?

  • Networking Like a Pro, 2nd Edition

  • World's Best-Known Marketing Secret

  • Healing Begins in the Kitchen: The Misner Plan

  • Avoiding the Networking Disconnect

  • 29% Solution     It's not "net-sit" or "net-eat," it's "net-WORK"

  • Business Networking and Sex (Not What You Think)     It's no surprise that communicating with the opposite sex can be tricky. Finally, help is here.

  • Masters of Success     Proven Techniques for Achieving Success in Business and Life

  • Truth or Delusion?     Busting Networking's Biggest Myths
